About Gravar
About

Gravar is a browser-based video transcription workspace for detailed interaction analysis. It is designed for timed transcript construction while viewing video, inspecting the audio waveform, and recording line-level talk/action notes.

The app runs locally in the browser as a single HTML file. Project data is saved separately as JSON, with optional TSV/CSV export for use in spreadsheets or other analysis tools.

Major functions include video loading, waveform navigation, transcript rows with Start/End/Speaker fields, editable transcript pop-out, video pop-out, transcript pop-out, symbol insertion, autosave, TSV/CSV/RTF export, and diagnostics.

Help
Help / User Manual

Gravar help guide and user manual

Gravar is a browser-based workspace for detailed video/audio transcription and interaction analysis. It combines synchronized media playback, transcript timing, waveform inspection, transcript-linked coding, comments, group/span indicators, pop-out displays, project saving, and structured exports in a single local HTML application.

Basic workflow

  1. Open Files → Import and load a primary video, optional V2, WAV/audio, TSV, ELAN TSV, or saved project.
  2. Use Files → Project → Folder when browser support is available. The Project folder is used for project JSON, autosaves, ordinary exports, and frame clips.
  3. Build transcript rows in the bottom timing/transcript workspace. Each row keeps Start, End, Duration, Speaker, Transcript, Code, Line Note, group-span membership, and child-line metadata together.
  4. Use Video and Wave for zooming, panning, scrubbing, waveform navigation, timing calibration, drag-duration measurement, and waveform-based row creation.
  5. Use the right pane for Code, Line Note, Project Note, Symbols, and Group tools.
  6. Save the project JSON often. The saved project includes the active Code, Symbols, and Group palettes, styled code markers, group ranges, comments, project note, transcript styles, and child-line tags.
  7. Use Files → Export for TSV, XLS, ELAN, RTF, Project Note, Comments, and Transcript+Comment. Use Video → V-Clip for start/end-time JSON specifications for external clip makers.

Workspace map

  • Left pane: Files, Wave, Video, Transcript, About, Comment, and Help menus.
  • Video display: primary V1 and optional synchronized V2. Both can be independently zoomed and panned; either can be scrubbed to synchronize media time.
  • Waveform: audio visualization, click-to-seek, plain-drag duration measurement, Command/Ctrl-drag interval row creation with Insert menu, two-finger horizontal slide paging, amplitude display, frame window selection, temporary nudge, and saved calibration.
  • Timing pane: row number, Start, End, Duration, and Speaker fields.
  • Transcript pane: transcript text, right-side Code/Note markers, and left-side Group hairline gutter.
  • Right pane: Code, Line Note, Project Note, Symbols, and Group tools.
  • Persistent controls: timing, line management, playback, transport, Follow, Delete, and frame-step buttons.

Files, projects, autosave, and imports

  • Project: Load, Save, Close, and Folder manage Gravar project JSON and the Project folder. Current Codes, Symbols, Groups, Code styles, and Group definitions are saved in the project.
  • Codes & Symbols: import/export reusable Code and Symbols JSON libraries. These shared-library files are separate from the Project folder, while every project still embeds its current palettes.
  • Import TSV: loads standard transcription TSV files. Gravar recognizes Start/End/Transcript headers, avoids misclassifying them as ELAN TSV, and sorts imported rows chronologically by Start time.
  • Load ELAN TSV: imports ELAN-style tab-delimited exports when onset/offset fields are truly time values.
  • Append project: appends rows from another Gravar project while preserving row data, notes, code values, group memberships, and timing.
  • Autosave: defaults to off. The interval defaults to 10 minutes and Keep saves defaults to 1. Turn autosave on with the checkbox in Files → Auto. After an autosave, 5 minutes without typing, clicking, pointer activity, or video playback pauses autosave; activity resumes it automatically.

Video tools and video pop-out

  • Fr-Clip: captures a PNG frame from V1, V2, or both videos. It does not save the project file.
  • V-Clip: saves a JSON clip specification from the selected transcript interval. The spec now includes FFmpeg-ready crop/scale/filter recipes for the current zoomed/panned video view and video display adjustments. Filenames begin with V-CLIP, then the start and stop times, then the original file name. The first V-Clip export in a session asks for a V-Clip folder used only for V-Clip specs.
  • V1 visible / V2 visible: show or hide videos in the main video pane without changing timing or playback synchronization.
  • Video adjustments: Bright, Contrast, Color, and Red/Green/Blue sliders apply live display filters to V1/V2 in the main video pane and video pop-out. These settings are saved in the project and used for Fr-Clip image capture.
  • Video Pop: toggles the muted synchronized video display on and off. Audio and timing remain controlled by the main app.
  • Stack: stacks V1 above V2 in both the main video pane and the video pop-out. When off, V1 and V2 display side-by-side.
  • Running-time strip: the video pop-out has a bottom strip showing current time, progress, and total duration.
  • Video gestures: Command/Ctrl-drag zooms, plain drag pans after zooming, Command/Ctrl-Option/Alt-drag scrubs, and double-click resets zoom/pan. Tooltips in the video pop-out describe these gestures for V1, V2, and the time strip.

Waveform and calibration

  • Click-to-seek: click the waveform to move the media clock to that point in the visible waveform frame.
  • Plain waveform drag: drag across the waveform to scrub the video while measuring the interval. Video scrub updates are paced by the current Vspeed setting for more precise control. Gravar displays the duration on a compact bar above the bottom scrub area and copies the duration in seconds to the clipboard when the drag is released.
  • Command/Ctrl waveform drag: drag across the waveform while holding Command/Ctrl to scrub the video at the current Vspeed pace. On release, Gravar copies the dragged interval timecode to the clipboard.
  • Amp− / Amp+: adjust waveform height only.
  • Frame: selects whole-video or fixed display windows such as 10 sec, 5 sec, or 2 sec.
  • Temporary nudge: moves the displayed waveform without saving the correction.
  • Absolute / Point / Clear: save an absolute offset, add calibration points for drift, or clear saved calibration.
  • Calibration card: the Waveform calibration controls use left and right blue hairlines for visual separation.

Transcript editing and timing

  • Start / End: Command/Ctrl-[ sets Start; Command/Ctrl-] sets End. Command/Ctrl-click a Start or End field seeks to that time; Command/Ctrl-Option/Alt-click inserts current media time.
  • line↓ / line↑ / Delete: Command/Ctrl-Return adds a row below; Command/Ctrl-Shift-Return adds a row above; Command/Ctrl-Delete or Command/Ctrl-Backspace deletes the active row. The same commands work in the transcript pop-out.
  • Merge: merges selected contiguous rows. Comment rows and child metadata are handled separately from ordinary transcript row text.
  • Drag reorder: drag the line-number cell up or down to reposition a row. All row data moves with it and line numbers renumber automatically.
  • sort-Start: in Transcript → Find & Replace, sorts rows by Start time; blank Start rows move to the bottom and same-time rows keep their order.
  • Speaker field: right-justified with 10 px right padding. Speaker italic/color styling is inherited visually by the associated transcript line.
  • Speaker color menu: Command/Ctrl-click Speaker text to open a six-color menu. This works in the main transcript and transcript pop-out.
  • Child mode: when Child tiers is checked, italicized Speaker rows are tagged as child tiers linked to the just-prior primary line and inherit that primary line’s Start/End/Duration.
  • Transcript text: remains plain transcript content. Speaker styling affects visual display and child tagging; it does not convert transcript text itself into stored rich text.

Transcript pop-out

  • Text Pop / Front / Close: open, bring forward, or close the editable transcript pop-out.
  • Hide main transcript: in Transcript → Pop-out, this checkbox appears only while the transcript pop-out is open. It hides the full main transcript workspace, including Start/End/Duration/Speaker timing fields, because those fields remain available in the pop-out.
  • Bidirectional sync: edits in the pop-out update the main transcript, and edits in the main transcript update the pop-out.
  • Style parity: the pop-out displays Code markers, Note markers, Group hairlines, Speaker style inheritance, child timing inheritance, row selection, active/follow states, Bands, Border, Underline settings, and transcript font size.
  • Speaker styling: the pop-out has an italic button, color selector, Command/Ctrl-I for italic, and Command/Ctrl-click Speaker color menu. Transcript lines inherit Speaker style visually in both windows.
  • Structural commands: Command/Ctrl-Return, Command/Ctrl-Shift-Return, Command/Ctrl-Delete/Backspace, Command/Ctrl-[, Command/Ctrl-], merge, drag reorder, and sort-Start work from the pop-out and force a clean sync when row structure changes.
  • Tooltips: the pop-out toolbar, Speaker fields, timing fields, transcript fields, group hairlines, code markers, note markers, and line-number drag handles include hover titles.

Code styles and transcript markers

  • Code edit mode: Code buttons and section headers can receive colors and shapes. Codes inherit a section color/shape until a new section or an individual code override.
  • Selected Code buttons: selected buttons use a border highlight in the code’s assigned/inherited color.
  • Transcript markers: styled Code shapes appear on the right side of transcript rows. Click a Code icon to show its name; Command/Ctrl-click changes or deletes that Code; Command/Ctrl-Option/Alt-click opens the Code palette to add another Code. The first selected code is farthest right; later selected codes extend leftward.
  • Legacy preservation: existing coded rows with no assigned style still show a fallback red-circle marker so old project coding remains visible.
  • Line Note marker: a small black diamond with a red top tip indicates a Line Note.

Group hairlines

  • Group pane: defines group buttons and section headers. Group items can be colored like codes, and section colors are inherited by following Group buttons.
  • Applying groups: select one or more transcript rows and click a Group button. A colored vertical hairline appears in the left group gutter spanning the selected rows.
  • Toggle behavior: each Group can produce only one hairline per transcript row. Re-clicking the same Group on already marked selected rows removes that Group from those rows.
  • Stable slots: the same Group always appears in the same horizontal slot, so discontinuous stretches line up vertically.
  • Expandable gutter: the gutter remains compact for up to three hairlines and expands when more Group lines are needed.
  • Tooltip: hovering over a group hairline shows the Group label.

Comments, Line Notes, Project Notes, and Symbols

  • Comments: Add comment inserts a light-red Comment row. Show/Hide changes visibility without deleting comments; Every 1m/5m/10m adds interval anchors.
  • Line Note: row-level analytic note for the active transcript row. It supports rich text for project save/RTF export.
  • Project Note: project-level note independent of transcript rows. Fr-Clip captures can add links here when saved to the Project folder.
  • Symbols: reusable symbol palette. In edit mode, symbols and section dividers can be added, deleted, reordered, described, imported, and exported.

Shortcuts by work location

LocationShortcutAction
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-click transcript textPlay the active row or selected row range once.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-Option/Alt-click transcript textRepeat the active row or selected row range until Escape or Shift-Space.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-Option/Alt-click transcript textIf multiple transcript rows are selected, play from the first selected Start through the last selected End and stop; otherwise play from that row’s Start time.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-[Set Start for the active/focused row.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-]Set End for the active/focused row.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-ReturnAdd a row below.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-Shift-ReturnAdd a row above.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-Delete or Command/Ctrl-BackspaceDelete the active/focused row.
Transcript rowCommand/Ctrl-MMerge selected contiguous rows.
Speaker fieldCommand/Ctrl-clickOpen Speaker color menu.
Speaker fieldCommand/Ctrl-IToggle italic in the transcript pop-out Speaker field.
PlaybackShift-SpacePlay/pause without typing a space into a transcript field.
Play buttonCommand/Ctrl-clickOpen playback-speed menu.
VideoCommand/Ctrl-dragZoom the video under the pointer.
VideoPlain drag after zoomingPan the zoomed video.
VideoCommand/Ctrl-Option/Alt-dragScrub video time.
VideoDouble-clickReset zoom and pan.
Video pop-outFToggle fullscreen.
Video pop-out attached controlsShift-Space; Command/Ctrl-Left/Right; Command/Ctrl-Shift-Left/RightPlay/pause, step by 0.1 sec, or jump by 1 sec from the pop-out controls.
Video pop-out waveformClick; drag; Command/Ctrl-dragSeek, scrub/measure, or create a transcript row from the selected interval while viewing the pop-out video.
WaveformPlain dragMeasure an interval, scrub at Vspeed pace without waveform auto-centering, and copy its duration to the clipboard on release.
WaveformCommand/Ctrl-dragCreate a new transcript row from the dragged interval with Start, End, and Duration; the waveform frame stays fixed during the drag.
WaveformTwo-finger horizontal slideMove the visible waveform window forward/back by 75% of the current waveform frame.
Shortcuts

Shortcuts

Shortcut convention: Use Command on Mac and Ctrl on PC unless a shortcut below names a different modifier.

Pane and workspace controls

  • Play/Pause: Shift + Space toggles playback.
  • Transport scrubbing: Command/Ctrl + ←/→ moves by 0.10 seconds; Command/Ctrl + Shift + ←/→ jumps by 1.0 second.
  • Play button: Command/Ctrl-click toggles play/pause; Command-Option/Ctrl-Alt-click starts from the focused row Start; double-click resets speed.
  • Speed menus: Command-Option-click or Ctrl-Alt-click opens playback speed; double-click frame-speed indicator resets frame shift.
  • Undo: Command/Ctrl + Z.

Transcript area

  • Add/delete rows: Command/Ctrl + Return below; Command/Ctrl + Shift + Return above; Command/Ctrl + Delete or Command/Ctrl + Backspace deletes the active row.
  • Set times: Command/Ctrl + [ sets Start; Command/Ctrl + ] sets End.
  • Start/End fields: Command/Ctrl-click seeks; Command-Option/Ctrl-Alt-click inserts current time.
  • Transcript field: Command/Ctrl-click plays the row or selected row range once; Command-Option/Ctrl-Alt-click repeats the row or selected row range until Escape or Shift-Space.
  • Selection/editing: Shift-click selects a range; Command/Ctrl + M merges selected rows; symbol clicks insert at the active cursor when symbol edit mode is off.

Video screen and waveform

  • Waveform: click to seek; plain drag scrubs, shows the selected duration, and copies duration on release; Command/Ctrl-drag scrubs and creates a transcript row from the dragged Start/End interval.
  • Video gestures: Command/Ctrl-drag zooms; plain drag pans a zoomed video; Command-Option/Ctrl-Alt-drag scrubs; double-click resets zoom/pan.

Pop-out windows

  • Shared transport: arrow scrubbing/jumps and Shift + Space control the main media from pop-outs.
  • Video pop-out: plain Space is suppressed to avoid detached-video drift; F toggles fullscreen.
  • Video pop-out attached controls: when the Controls checkbox is on, the pop-out buttons mirror the main transport row. Use Shift + Space for play/pause, Command/Ctrl + ←/→ for 0.1-second steps, and Command/Ctrl + Shift + ←/→ for 1-second jumps.
  • Video pop-out waveform: click seeks, drag scrubs/measures, Command/Ctrl-drag creates a transcript row from the selected interval, and a two-finger horizontal slide pages the waveform/video forward or backward.
  • Text pop-out: uses the same row selection, segment playback, timestamp, and symbol-insertion logic as the main transcript.
Time: 00:00.000 / 00:00.000
